source_jobs.title: Founding Analytics Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Building the financial operating system for healthcare, and bringing the joy back to healthcare by fixing the financial chaos behind it.

The healthcare payment system is a complex and inefficient maze - healthcare practices leave $125 billion in revenue uncollected each year, lost in the chaos of fragmented financial data, manual workflows, and opaque payer systems. This financial uncertainty leaves practices struggling to stay afloat, while valuable revenue slips through the cracks.

Joyful Health is building the AI-powered financial operating system for healthcare practices. Our mission is to bring the joy back to running a private practice by simplifying financial operations so providers can focus on patient care. We spent 10 months working as fractional CFOs for a dozen practices, doing this work side by side with providers as we developed our product.

We just announced our $22M Series A led by CRV and world-class investors including the founders of MongoDB & KAYAK.

This role is full-time. We’re looking for someone NYC-based who is open to coming into the office 3 days a week.

The base pay range for this role is $120,000–$235,000 per year.




ROLE DESCRIPTION

We're looking for a talented and ambitious founding analytics engineer with 5+ years of experience in building data models and analytics infrastructure who's excited to create the analytics foundation from the ground up. You'll be the first analytics hire, responsible for designing and implementing the entire analytics infrastructure, defining our metrics framework, and transforming complex healthcare financial data into actionable insights.

Healthcare financial data is notoriously complex and fragmented - each practice has unique systems, inconsistent data structures, and limited visibility into their financial performance. This has meant that very few solutions have emerged in the market that actually help practices make informed decisions about their business. We've begun to crack this challenge, and now we need someone to build the analytics infrastructure and system design that will enable both our team and our customers to make data-driven decisions at scale.

As the founding analytics engineer, you'll have an outsized impact on the company's trajectory, establishing the analytics patterns and practices that will scale with us. This is a rare opportunity to build something from scratch - you'll own the entire analytics stack, from infrastructure and data modeling to visualization and metrics definitions. You should be comfortable (and excited!) about working in a fast-paced, early-stage startup environment where you'll be able to wear many hats and take on new challenges as they arise.

This role is full-time. We're looking for candidates based in NYC.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build the analytics infrastructure from scratch - design and implement the entire analytics stack, including the data warehouse architecture, transformation layer, semantic layer, and visualization tools.

 - Establish foundational data models - create robust, scalable data models that transform raw healthcare financial data into clear, actionable metrics and insights. Define the modeling patterns and conventions that the team will follow as we scale.

 - Design the metrics framework - work with leadership to define key business metrics, establish metrics definitions, and build the infrastructure to track them reliably. Own how we measure success across the organization.

 - Create self-service analytics capabilities - build intuitive dashboards and analytics tools that enable both internal teams and customers to understand revenue cycle performance, identify uncollected revenue, and track financial health.

 - Drive the analytics roadmap - partner closely with product, engineering, and customer success to prioritize analytics initiatives, define requirements, and ensure we're building the right solutions.

 - Build data transformation pipelines - implement modern ELT/ETL patterns usi

source_jobs.title: Founding Business Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT JOYFUL HEALTH

Building the financial operating system for healthcare, and bringing the joy back to healthcare by fixing the financial chaos behind it.

The healthcare payment system is a complex and inefficient maze - healthcare practices leave $125 billion in revenue uncollected each year, lost in the chaos of fragmented financial data, manual workflows, and opaque payer systems. This financial uncertainty leaves practices struggling to stay afloat, while valuable revenue slips through the cracks.

Joyful Health is building the AI-powered financial operating system for healthcare practices. Our mission is to bring the joy back to running a private practice by simplifying financial operations so providers can focus on patient care. We spent 10 months working as fractional CFOs for a dozen practices, doing this work side by side with providers as we developed our product.



We just announced our $22M Series A led by CRV and world-class investors including the founders of MongoDB & KAYAK.
🚀 We have an enormous opportunity in front of us. The broken healthcare payment ecosystem impacts practices of all sizes, and the opportunity to make a real difference is massive.

If you’re passionate about empowering medical practices and excited to tackle one of the most important (& challenging!) problems in healthcare, we’d love to meet you.




THE ROLE

Role Overview

We're looking for a Business Development Representative (BDR) to own our outbound prospecting motion at Joyful Health. As our Founding BDR, you'll be the tip of the spear for pipeline creation, reaching outpatient healthcare practices across the country and setting qualified meetings for our sales team.

This is a high-impact role for someone early in their sales career who is hungry, coachable, and genuinely excited about the healthcare space. You'll develop deep expertise in RCM and healthcare finance while helping build the outbound playbook that fuels our next phase of growth.



This role is full-time. We are looking for someone NYC-based who can be in the office 3 days per week.




WHAT YOU'LL DO…

 - Own outbound prospecting.

 - Use AI tools (Claude, etc.) to research accounts faster, personalize at scale, and multiply your output. This isn't a "nice to have," it's how we expect you to work.

 - Identify, research, and reach outpatient healthcare practices through cold calls, email sequences, and LinkedIn outreach.

 - Build and work cadences that reflect Joyful's ICP and messaging strategy.

 - Maintain high activity volume without sacrificing personalization.

 - Work our bottom-up channels (events, community, partnerships) to qualify inbound interest and set discovery calls.

 - Distinguish prospects who are "open to a conversation" from those with real, urgent pain.

 - Set and hand off meetings with qualified stakeholders

 - Book and transition high-quality discovery calls to an Account Executive with clear context and handoff notes.

 - Take ownership of show rate and meeting quality, not just volume.

 - Maintain CRM hygiene - keep HubSpot updated and accurate so the team has real visibility into pipeline and activity.

 - Log every touchpoint and outcome. Use data to self-correct.

 - Share market intelligence.

 - Surface patterns from prospect conversations that help refine ICP, messaging, and positioning.

 - Build healthcare expertise

 - Learn the RCM and billing landscape so you can speak credibly with practice administrators, revenue cycle managers, and CFOs.

 - Understand the economics of how practices get paid and where revenue slips through the cracks.




WE’D LOVE TO HEAR FROM YOU IF…

You're passionate about Joyful's mission

 - You want to solve real problems for healthcare providers and are motivated by fixing the broken systems in healthcare.

You have 1-2+ years of sales or BDR/SDR experience in a high growth startup environment

 - Track record of consistent outbound activity: cold calls, email sequences, and multi-touch cadences

source_jobs.title: AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Building the financial operating system for healthcare, and bringing the joy back to healthcare by fixing the financial chaos behind it.

The healthcare payment system is a complex and inefficient maze - healthcare practices leave $125 billion in revenue uncollected each year, lost in the chaos of fragmented financial data, manual workflows, and opaque payer systems. This financial uncertainty leaves practices struggling to stay afloat, while valuable revenue slips through the cracks.

Joyful Health is building the AI-powered financial operating system for healthcare practices. Our mission is to bring the joy back to running a private practice by simplifying financial operations so providers can focus on patient care. We spent 10 months working as fractional CFOs for a dozen practices, doing this work side by side with providers as we developed our product.

We just announced our $22M Series A led by CRV and world-class investors including the founders of MongoDB & KAYAK.

🚀 We have an enormous opportunity in front of us. The broken healthcare payment ecosystem impacts practices of all sizes, and the opportunity to make a real difference is massive.

This role is full-time. We’re looking for someone NYC-based who is open to coming into the office 3 days a week.

The base pay range for this role is $150,000–$275,000 per year.





ROLE DESCRIPTION

We're looking for a talented and ambitious machine learning engineer with 5+ years of experience building production AI systems who's excited to solve one of the toughest challenges in healthcare - automatically resolving claim denials. You'll be the technical lead for our agentic AI platform that autonomously researches, understands, and resolves insurance claim denials, helping healthcare practices recover millions in lost revenue.

Insurance claim denials cost healthcare practices $125 billion annually, and resolving them requires deep expertise in medical billing, payer rules, and claim documentation. Today, billing managers spend hours manually researching each denial, gathering supporting documentation, and crafting appeals. We're building an agentic AI system that automates this entire workflow - from denial classification and root cause analysis, to evidence gathering and appeal letter generation.

As a machine learning engineer, you'll architect and build the intelligent agents that power this system. You'll design multi-agent workflows that collaborate to interpret complex denial reasons, retrieve relevant clinical and billing information through RAG systems, reason about payer policies, and generate compelling appeals. This is a greenfield opportunity to build a production agentic system that will directly impact the financial health of healthcare practices.

You should be comfortable (and excited!) about working in a fast-paced, early-stage startup environment where you'll be able to wear many hats and take on new challenges as they arise. This is a great opportunity for someone who wants to work on something extremely challenging at the intersection of AI and healthcare.

This role is full-time. We're looking for candidates based in NYC.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design and build the architecture for our agentic AI system that autonomously resolves insurance claim denials

 - Develop specialized AI agents for denial classification, root cause analysis, evidence retrieval, policy reasoning, and appeal generation

 - Implement multi-agent orchestration frameworks that coordinate complex workflows across research, decision-making, and document generation

 - Build and optimize RAG systems to retrieve relevant clinical documentation, billing records, and payer policy information

 - Create evaluation frameworks and feedback loops to continuously improve agent performance and reliability

 - Design prompt engineering strategies and fine-tuning approaches to optimize LLM behavior for healthcare billing workflows

 - Work closely with billing managers to understand denial resoluti

source_jobs.title: Founding Infra Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT JOYFUL HEALTH

Building the financial operating system for healthcare, and bringing the joy back to healthcare by fixing the financial chaos behind it.

The healthcare payment system is a complex and inefficient maze - healthcare practices leave $125 billion in revenue uncollected each year, lost in the chaos of fragmented financial data, manual workflows, and opaque payer systems. This financial uncertainty leaves practices struggling to stay afloat, while valuable revenue slips through the cracks.

Joyful Health is building the AI-powered financial operating system for healthcare practices. Our mission is to bring the joy back to running a private practice by simplifying financial operations so providers can focus on patient care. We spent 10 months working as fractional CFOs for a dozen practices, doing this work side by side with providers as we developed our product.

We just announced our $22M Series A led by CRV and world-class investors including the founders of MongoDB & KAYAK.

🚀 We have an enormous opportunity in front of us. The broken healthcare payment ecosystem impacts practices of all sizes, and the opportunity to make a real difference is massive.

This role is full-time. We’re looking for someone NYC-based who is open to coming into the office 3 days a week.

The base pay range for this role is $150,000–$275,000 per year.




ROLE DESCRIPTION

We’re hiring a founding Infra engineer with deep experience across Kubernetes, cloud infrastructure, CI/CD, and production-grade reliability. You’ll own the foundation that powers everything we build—our deployment architecture, service mesh, observability stack, and core platform reliability.

Our platform ingests, reconciles, and analyzes large volumes of messy financial data from every type of healthcare system imaginable. Keeping that pipeline stable, secure, and scalable is mission-critical. In a landscape where every customer’s environment is different, highly repeatable infrastructure and robust automation are differentiators.

You’ll design, build, and operate the core infrastructure that allows us to ship fast, stay reliable, and onboard customers seamlessly. As one of the earliest engineers on the team, you’ll shape our architecture, engineering practices, and culture from day one.

This is a full-time role, based in NYC.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Accelerate the entire engineering team by building novel tooling, automation, and proven infra best practices that dramatically increase development velocity

 - Design and maintain high-leverage CI/CD workflows (GitHub Actions) that make shipping safe, fast, and frustration-free

 - Own our Kubernetes-based platform end-to-end: architecture, deployment strategy, scaling, and overall reliability

 - Build a stable, automated infrastructure layer around our Python services, ingestion pipelines, and compute workloads

 - Implement robust observability (metrics, logs, tracing) and build a clean operational picture of system health

 - Establish strong security foundations: secrets management, IAM hardening, system-level policies, and compliance-friendly patterns

 - Improve and optimize PostgreSQL performance, monitoring, and operational stability across workloads

 - Drive the team’s infrastructure roadmap and ensure our architecture supports rapid onboarding and growth

 - Build internal tooling that reduces complexity, eliminates manual ops, and gives engineers “superpowers”

 - Collaborate closely with engineering and product to ensure that infra choices enable—not constrain—our product velocity


WE’D LOVE TO HEAR FROM YOU IF…

 - You’re energized by Joyful Health’s mission—building tools that help providers take control of their financial health

 - You want to shape foundational architecture at an early-stage startup and influence technical decisions for years

 - You thrive in ambiguous environments and take pride in owning problems end-to-end

 - You value craftsmanship, reliability, and clean abstractions—i

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:

We spent 10 months working as fractional CFOs for a dozen practices, doing this work side by side with providers as we developed our product.

We just announced our $22M Series A led by CRV and world-class investors including the founders of MongoDB & KAYAK.

🚀 We have an enormous opportunity in front of us. The broken healthcare payment ecosystem impacts practices of all sizes, and the opportunity to make a real difference is massive.

This role is full-time. We’re looking for someone NYC-based who is open to coming into the office 3 days a week.

The base pay range for this role is $140,000-$170,000 per year + uncapped commission. Base pay offered may vary depending on job and industry-related knowledge, skills, and experience.




ROLE DESCRIPTION

We’re looking for a full-cycle Account Executive to join Joyful Health and us scale our offering to more provider groups across the country.

This is a rare opportunity to join a company at the moment when product-market pull is accelerating. Healthcare practices are actively searching for better ways to manage revenue and financial operations. Because of this, we frequently see more demand than we can currently support.

This role is designed for someone who wants to own meaningful revenue early and help build the foundation of the sales team at Joyful.

You'll:

 - Sell a product solving one of the most painful operational problems in healthcare

 - Work on mid-market and enterprise deals with multi-location healthcare practices

 - Own the full sales cycle — from sourcing opportunities to closing contracts

 - Help shape the sales motion and go-to-market strategy as we scale

For the right person, this role offers the opportunity to have outsized impact on Joyful’s growth while building a career-defining track record in healthcare technology.

This role is full-time and based in NYC. We'll provide relocation assistance for the right candidate.




WHAT YOU'LL DO…


OWN THE FULL SALES CYCLE

 - Prospect, qualify, demo, negotiate, and close deals with mid-market and enterprise healthcare practices

 - Run consultative sales processes with multiple stakeholders including practice operators, billing leaders, and clinical leadership

 - Guide prospects from initial discovery through contract execution


BUILD AND MANAGE YOUR PIPELINE

 - Develop your own outbound pipeline through targeted prospecting and relationship building

 - Convert inbound opportunities generated through our community, partnerships, and events

 - Maintain a strong pipeline that supports consistent quota attainment


CLOSE COMPLEX HEALTHCARE DEALS

 - Lead thoughtful discovery conversations around financial operations and revenue cycle challenges

 - Run tailored product demonstrations and ROI discussions

 - Navigate multi-stakeholder purchasing processes and procurement workflows


HELP SHAPE OUR GO-TO-MARKET MOTION

 - Provide feedback on messaging, positioning, and pricing based on real customer conversations

 - Collaborate with marketing and leadership on sales materials, case studies, and ROI narratives

 - Share insights that help us refine our sales motion as we grow


BUILD RELATIONSHIPS IN THE HEALTHCARE ECOSYSTEM

 - Develop relationships within the RCM and healthcare operations comm

source_jobs.title: RCM Architect
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT JOYFUL HEALTH

Building the financial operating system for healthcare — and bringing the joy back to healthcare by fixing the financial chaos behind it.

The healthcare payment system is a complex and inefficient maze. Healthcare practices leave $125 billion in revenue uncollected each year, lost in the chaos of fragmented financial data, manual workflows, and opaque payer systems. This financial uncertainty leaves practices struggling to stay afloat while valuable revenue slips through the cracks.

Joyful Health is building an AI-powered financial operating system for healthcare practices. Our mission is to bring the joy back to running a private practice by simplifying financial operations so providers can focus on patient care. We spent 10 months working as fractional CFOs for a dozen practices, doing this work side by side with providers as we developed our product.



We just announced our $22M Series A led by CRV and world-class investors including the founders of MongoDB & KAYAK.

At the center of our model is the RCM Center of Excellence (CoE) — the layer that translates real-world billing complexity into scalable systems. If you’re excited about applying deep RCM expertise to fix a system that is fundamentally broken, we’d love to meet you.




THE ROLE

We are hiring an RCM Architect to serve as the RCM knowledge layer across the organization.

This is a high-leverage, cross-functional role that sits at the intersection of Sales, Customer Success & Operations, and Product & Engineering. You are the person who defines what correct RCM looks like — and ensures the organization is executing toward it.

This role is accountable for outcomes, not just artifacts.

RCM Architects are not measured by the quality of the document they produce — they are measured by whether the function, project, or account achieves its intended RCM result. You own the outcome.

You will be responsible for translating RCM complexity into clear strategy, structured workflows, and actionable insights — ensuring that:

 - We start customers correctly

 - We build the right product

 - We solve the right problems

 - The organization develops a compounding understanding of RCM patterns and solutions




WHAT YOU’LL DO

The RCM Architect is the connective tissue between Joyful's RCM expertise and every team that depends on it. On any given day, you might be deep in a customer's denial data identifying the root cause of a revenue leakage pattern, sitting alongside Engineering to validate whether a new agent workflow reflects how billing actually works, or building the SOP that a BPO team will use to execute against a new specialty.

This is not a role defined by a single function. You are embedded across Sales, Customer Success, Product, and Operations — not as a support resource, but as the person who sets the standard for what correct RCM looks like and ensures the organization is executing toward it. You own the outcome, not just the deliverable.

 - Audit claims and drive strategy — Review claims for accuracy of action taken, validate next-best-action decisions, and translate findings into systemic recommendations. You're not fixing one claim; you're identifying the pattern behind it.

 - Turn denial data into actionable workflows — Translate denial patterns into root causes and resolution pathways. Define standardized recovery strategies, escalation logic, and decision frameworks that hold up against real-world payer behavior.

 - Codify RCM knowledge at scale — Build and maintain the SOPs, specialty guides, and decision frameworks that allow the organization to execute at a high RCM standard without you in every room. You are building a system, not just answering questions.

 - Set every new customer up for success — Analyze denial rates, aging, payer mix, and workflow gaps at onboarding. Deliver a data-backed strategic action plan to the RCSM so every account starts with a clear path forward.

 - Enable the organization — Support new hir

source_jobs.title: Revenue Recovery Specialist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT JOYFUL HEALTH

Building the financial operating system for healthcare, and bringing the joy back to healthcare by fixing the financial chaos behind it.

The healthcare payment system is a complex and inefficient maze - healthcare practices leave $125 billion in revenue uncollected each year, lost in the chaos of fragmented financial data, manual workflows, and opaque payer systems. This financial uncertainty leaves practices struggling to stay afloat, while valuable revenue slips through the cracks.

Joyful Health is building the AI-powered financial operating system for healthcare practices. Our mission is to bring the joy back to running a private practice by simplifying financial operations so providers can focus on patient care. We just announced our $22M Series A led by CRV and world-class investors including the founders of MongoDB & KAYAK.



🚀 We have an enormous opportunity in front of us. The broken healthcare payment ecosystem impacts practices of all sizes, and the opportunity to make a real difference is massive.

If you're passionate about empowering medical practices and excited to tackle one of the most important (& challenging!) problems in healthcare, we'd love to meet you.




THE ROLE


WHY THIS ROLE EXISTS

Denials, rejections, and aging AR are where practices quietly bleed revenue - and where most RCM operations get stuck doing slow, manual, repetitive work. This is the hardest, highest-leverage work in the revenue cycle, and it's exactly where Joyful Health's AI-native tooling changes the game.

We are looking to hire a Revenue Recovery Specialist: a deeply experienced RCM professional who will work denials, rejections, and outstanding AR for our customers - not by grinding through claims by hand, but by pairing your expertise with state-of-the-art AI RCM tooling to resolve issues faster and at higher volume than traditional RCM ever could.

This is not simply a billing specialist seat. You'll be a recovery expert who knows how payers think, how claims get denied, and how to get them paid - and you'll use Joyful Health's platform to do it at a scale and speed that wouldn't be possible anywhere else. Your work directly delivers on the value we promise our customers, and your expertise directly shapes the product we're building.

This is a remote, full-time role.




WHAT MAKES THIS DIFFERENT

This is a human-in-the-loop role at an AI-native healthcare company - which means two things define how you'll work:

Recovery is the job - throughput and quality are the goal. Your core mandate is clear: recover revenue for our customers by resolving the denials, rejections, and AR that practices can't. You'll work the hardest claims, and you'll have state-of-the-art AI RCM tooling that strips out the manual pains so your expertise goes where it actually moves the needle. We measure what matters in recovery - dollars recovered, denials overturned, days-to-resolution, and the quality of the work - not touches or call volume.

You use our product to win for customers, and that fuels the loop. You'll combine Joyful Health's tooling with each customer's priorities to get claims paid - and because you're working real claims at the frontier, what you experience becomes valuable signal. The patterns you surface from the recovery frontline flow directly to our RCM Center of Excellence and Engineering teams, giving them exactly what they need to build smarter, more sophisticated tooling. Your frontline insight is one of the most important inputs into how the whole engine gets better.




WHAT YOU'LL DO…

 - Own denials, rejections, and outstanding AR for our customers: Work the full recovery lifecycle - diagnosing root causes, reworking and resubmitting claims, drafting appeals, and chasing aging AR to resolution.

 - Pair deep RCM judgment with AI-native tooling: Use Joyful Health's state-of-the-art platform to resolve claims at a speed and scale traditional RCM can't match - applying your expertise where human judgme

source_jobs.title: Business Operations Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT JOYFUL HEALTH

Building the financial operating system for healthcare, and bringing the joy back to healthcare by fixing the financial chaos behind it.

The healthcare payment system is a complex and inefficient maze - healthcare practices leave $125 billion in revenue uncollected each year, lost in the chaos of fragmented financial data, manual workflows, and opaque payer systems. This financial uncertainty leaves practices struggling to stay afloat, while valuable revenue slips through the cracks.

Joyful Health is building the AI-powered financial operating system for healthcare practices. Our mission is to bring the joy back to running a private practice by simplifying financial operations so providers can focus on patient care. We spent 10 months working as fractional CFOs for a dozen practices, doing this work side by side with providers as we developed our product.



We just announced our $22M Series A led by CRV and world-class investors including the founders of MongoDB & KAYAK.

🚀 We have an enormous opportunity in front of us. The broken healthcare payment ecosystem impacts practices of all sizes, and the opportunity to make a real difference is massive.

If you're passionate about empowering medical practices and excited to tackle one of the most important (& challenging!) problems in healthcare, we'd love to meet you.




THE ROLE


WHY THIS ROLE EXISTS

As Joyful Health continues to grow our Revenue Recovery service line, our post-sales operation is outgrowing the tools and workflows that got us here. We need someone who owns the connective tissue - the systems, automations, and reporting - that make business ops actually run.

The Business Ops Lead builds and owns the automation and tooling infrastructure behind our post-sales operation. You'll partner closely with Operations leadership so they can stay focused on outcomes, customer account performance, forecasting, and our product — while you make sure the machine underneath runs beautifully.

This is a builder role for someone who thinks in systems, gets excited about standing up process from scratch, and wants to become the go-to person for AI and automation across the entire ops org.



This role is full-time. We're looking for someone NYC-based who is open to coming into the office 3 days a week.




WHAT MAKES THIS DIFFERENT

This isn't a traditional ops seat at a company with mature systems. Two principles shape how we think about this role:

You own the connective tissue, not a single tool. Most ops hires inherit a stack and optimize within it. You'll be making the actual calls on how our CRM, ticketing, reporting, and automation layers fit together, and building the ones that don't exist yet! Your work shows up in how quickly the rest of the team can move.

AI and automation are core to the job, not a side project. We're an AI-native team across every function. Over time, this role grows into Joyful Health's ops automation czar — the person bringing AI and automation into how we onboard, bill, support, and report on customers.




WHAT YOU'LL DO…

 - Build out and own our post-sales CRM: Stand up a real system of record for customer operations so the team has one source of truth on every account.

 - Design our ticketing infrastructure: Own the email ticketing system and Airtable ticketing workflows, and shape how work flows through the team as we scale.

 - Build reporting and dashboards: Create the connective tissue between our ops data, our tooling, and how the team makes decisions. (Fair warning: reporting will live in a few different hands across ops — but you'll set the standard.)

 - Optimize workflows and tooling across ops: Be the unofficial ops automation czar — spotting friction, building automations, and continuously sharpening how the team operates.

 - Manage the systems behind onboarding and invoicing: Own the infrastructure that supports a smooth customer onboarding and billing experience.

 - Lead on AI and automation: Over time,

source_jobs.title: RCM Workflow Specialist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT JOYFUL HEALTH

Building the financial operating system for healthcare — and bringing the joy back to healthcare by fixing the financial chaos behind it.

The healthcare payment system is a complex and inefficient maze. Healthcare practices leave $125 billion in revenue uncollected each year, lost in the chaos of fragmented financial data, manual workflows, and opaque payer systems. This financial uncertainty leaves practices struggling to stay afloat while valuable revenue slips through the cracks.

Joyful Health is building an AI-powered financial operating system for healthcare practices. Our mission is to bring the joy back to running a private practice by simplifying financial operations so providers can focus on patient care. We spent 10 months working as fractional CFOs for a dozen practices, doing this work side by side with providers as we developed our product.



We just announced our $22M Series A led by CRV and world-class investors including the founders of MongoDB & KAYAK.

If you’re excited about combining real-world RCM expertise with product innovation, we’d love to meet you.




THE ROLE

The RCM Workflow Specialist sits at the intersection of revenue cycle operations and product intelligence.

This is not a traditional billing role.

You are responsible for two things that most billing professionals have never been asked to do together: 1) work real claims at an expert level and 2) translate that expertise into the structured data and direct feedback that powers Joyful’s AI product. Every action you take — every agent decision you review, every denial you analyze, every correction you document — directly improves how our system understands and solves revenue cycle problems.

You are both:

 - An expert-level RCM practitioner

 - A collaborator who shapes how our AI learns

This role is part of the RCM Center of Excellence (CoE) and partners directly with the Engineering and Product teams.



There are two distinct tracks of work in this role:

Track 1: Agent QA & Data Labeling: Structured, recurring. You review AI agent decisions on claims, label them correct or incorrect with rationale, and provide natural language corrections that feed directly into model training. The quality of your labels is what makes the model smarter. This requires genuine RCM expertise — many decisions are judgment calls, not rule lookups.

Track 2: Product UX & Collaboration: Collaborative, fluid. You work directly alongside Engineering to pressure-test features against real-world RCM workflows. You tell us what’s hard to use, what doesn’t reflect how billing actually works, and what’s missing. You push back — this is an active, opinionated role, not a passive observer.




WHAT YOU’LL DO


EXECUTE HIGH-QUALITY CLAIMS WORKFLOWS

 - Work claims across denial, A/R, and follow-up workflows with a focus on accuracy and decision quality — not volume

 - Perform investigation, correction, and resolution of claims

 - Interact with payer systems, portals, and call centers as needed

 - Get creative when standard paths don’t work — you find a way to get the claim resolved. There is no such thing as “we can’t do it.”


REVIEW AND LABEL AGENT DECISIONS

 - Review a statistical sample of AI agent–closed encounters each week and assess whether the agent’s action was correct, with a written rationale for your decision

 - Provide natural language corrections through the product interface (e.g., “We should have checked the payer portal before closing — there was a timely filing issue that needed a retro authorization”)

 - These corrections feed directly into model training — your expertise is what makes the system smarter

 - Flag patterns in where the agent consistently struggles or makes avoidable errors

 - Make confident calls on ambiguous scenarios — many of these are judgment calls, and sitting on the fence is not an option


STRUCTURE DATA THROUGH LABELING

 - Translate claim activity into standardized, structured workflow outputs

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:

We’re looking for a talented and ambitious backend engineer with 5+ years of experience in developing distributed pipelines who’s excited to solve one of the toughest challenges in healthcare—financial data interoperability. You’ll play a key role in shaping our data integration strategy and enabling us to onboard customers at scale.

Integrating the financial data of small healthcare practices—each with its own unique systems—has long been a roadblock in the industry. This has meant that very few solutions have emerged in the market that are actually able to solve the unique challenges of small medical practices (and even larger ones). We’ve begun to crack this challenge, and the work that we’ve done so far has enabled us to seamlessly onboard new data sources in days, not months. This entirely opens up a massive untapped market.

As a software engineer, you’ll have an outsized impact on the company’s trajectory, working on a critical problem that’s central to our success. This is a great opportunity for someone who wants to work on something extremely challenging and make a huge difference. You should be comfortable (and excited!) about working in a fast-paced, early-stage startup environment where you'll be able to wear many hats and take on new challenges as they arise.

This role is full-time. We’re looking for candidates based in NYC.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Drive the architecture, design, and implementation of complex pipelines for AI agents and data ingestion sources

 - Develop and scale our data integration infrastructure to connect seamlessly with a variety of healthcare systems.

 - Solve interoperability challenges to help healthcare practices recover lost revenue and understand their financial health.

 - Work closely with the engineering and product teams to improve onboarding speed and build a robust data strategy.

 - Actively contribute to building the team’s architectural vision and technical roadmap

 - Collaborate on code reviews and technical design documents to ensure code quality and distribute knowledge


WE’D LOVE TO HEAR FROM YOU IF…

 - You’re passionate about Joyful Health’s mission – you want to solve real, pressing problems for providers and put them in control of their businesses. You naturally empathize with users and enjoy designing solutions with their needs in mind.

 - You’re excited to shape the future of our engineering team and are excited to lead by example – as an early team member, you’ll have the chance to influence everything from our technical roadmap to our company culture. You have a radical sense of ownership with the ability and desire

The healthcare payment system is a complex and inefficient maze - healthcare practices leave $125 billion in revenue uncollected each year, lost in the chaos of fragmented financial data, manual workflows, and opaque payer systems. This financial uncertainty leaves practices struggling to stay afloat, while valuable revenue slips through the cracks.

Joyful Health is building the AI-powered financial operating system for healthcare practices. Our mission is to bring the joy back to running a private practice by simplifying financial operations so providers can focus on patient care. We spent 10 months working as fractional CFOs for a dozen practices, doing this work side by side with providers as we developed our product.

We just announced our $22M Series A led by CRV and world-class investors including the founders of MongoDB & KAYAK.

 - You’re passionate about Joyful Health’s mission – you want to solve real, pressing problems for providers and put them in control of their businesses. You naturally empathize with users and enjoy designing solutions with their needs in mind.

You have a radical sense of ownership with the ability and desire to own technical roadmaps and refine ambiguous problems. You also have a low ego, growth mindset, and desire to see the product and team scale with your impact.

 - You have a love for your craft and strong technical execution - you view coding as a craft, not just a task, and you’re excited about using technology to create meaningful impact. Your technical skills go beyond coding—you care about building a product that works at scale, with the user at the forefront. We care more about your drive, hustle, and passion than a college degree from a shiny institution or experience at name-brand companies.

 - You have 5+ years of experience in data engineering, with a focus on building data pipelines and integrations.
   
   - Experience working with healthcare data or solving interoperability challenges is a plus.
   
   - Proficiency in:
     
     - Python and database systems, with familiarity in performance and optimization for scale
     
     - Experience in distributed systems coordination and AI pipelines
     
     - Familiarity with big data technologies and comfortable working with massive datasets
     
     - CI/CD workflows such as Github actions

The healthcare payment system is a complex and inefficient maze - healthcare practices leave $125 billion in revenue uncollected each year, lost in the chaos of fragmented financial data, manual workflows, and opaque payer systems. This financial uncertainty leaves practices struggling to stay afloat, while valuable revenue slips through the cracks.

Joyful Health is building the AI-powered financial operating system for healthcare practices. Our mission is to bring the joy back to running a private practice by simplifying financial operations so providers can focus on patient care. We spent 10 months working as fractional CFOs for a dozen practices, doing this work side by side with providers as we developed our product.

We just announced our $22M Series A led by CRV and world-class investors including the founders of MongoDB & KAYAK.

🚀 We have an enormous opportunity in front of us. The broken healthcare payment ecosystem impacts practices of all sizes, and the opportunity to make a real difference is massive.

This role is full-time. We’re looking for someone NYC-based who is open to coming into the office 3 days a week.

The base pay range for this role is $150,000–$275,000 per year.




ROLE DESCRIPTION

We're looking for a talented and ambitious fullstack engineer with 3+ years of experience who's excited to build beautiful, intuitive user experiences that solve one of the toughest challenges in healthcare—helping providers understand and manage their financial operations. You'll play a key role in shipping features that delight users and make complex financial data accessible and actionable.

Healthcare providers are drowning in financial chaos, struggling to navigate opaque systems and fragmented data. We're building the operating system that brings clarity to this complexity. The interfaces and experiences you create will be the primary way practices interact with their financial data, make critical business decisions, and reclaim millions in lost revenue.

As a product engineer, you'll have an outsized impact on the company's trajectory, working directly on the features that define our product and drive customer delight. This is a great opportunity for someone who wants to own features end-to-end and make a huge difference. You should be comfortable (and excited!) about working in a fast-paced, early-stage startup environment where you'll be able to wear many hats and take on new challenges as they arise.

This role is full-time. We're looking for candidates based in NYC.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design and build intuitive, responsive user interfaces using React and TypeScript that make complex financial workflows simple and delightful

 - Own features end-to-end from concept to deployment, working across the full stack (React frontend, FastAPI backend) to ship quickly and iteratively

 - Collaborate closely with designers, product, and customers to deeply understand user needs and translate them into elegant, functional experiences

 - Build and refine core product features including dashboards, analytics visualizations, workflow tools, and AI-powered copilot interfaces

 - Drive improvements to our design system, component library, and frontend architecture to enable the team to move faster

 - Work with backend engineers to design clean APIs and data models that support great user experiences

 - Actively contribute to building the team's product vision, technical roadmap, and engineering culture


WE'D LOVE TO HEAR FROM YOU IF…

 - You're passionate about Joyful Health's mission – you want to solve real, pressing problems for providers and put them in control of their businesses. You naturally empathize with users and enjoy designing solutions with their needs in mind. You have strong product intuition and an eye for detail.

source_jobs.title: Chief of Staff
source_jobs.description:
Building the financial operating system for healthcare, and bringing the joy back to healthcare by fixing the financial chaos behind it.

The healthcare payment system is a complex and inefficient maze - healthcare practices leave $125 billion in revenue uncollected each year, lost in the chaos of fragmented financial data, manual workflows, and opaque payer systems. This financial uncertainty leaves practices struggling to stay afloat, while valuable revenue slips through the cracks.

Joyful Health is building the AI-powered financial operating system for healthcare practices. Our mission is to bring the joy back to running a private practice by simplifying financial operations so providers can focus on patient care. We spent 10 months working as fractional CFOs for a dozen practices, doing this work side by side with providers as we developed our product.

We just announced our $22M Series A led by CRV and world-class investors including the founders of MongoDB & KAYAK.

🚀 We have an enormous opportunity in front of us. The broken healthcare payment ecosystem impacts practices of all sizes, and the opportunity to make a real difference is massive.




ROLE DESCRIPTION

We’re looking for a Chief of Staff to partner closely with our CEO and leadership team as Joyful Health scales.

This is a senior, high-impact role, ideally for someone with prior Chief of Staff experience or experience operating in similarly broad, high-leverage roles. You’ll act as a force multiplier for the CEO and for the company — helping set direction, drive execution, and ensure that the most important work gets done exceptionally well.


WHAT THIS ROLE IS:

 - You’ll effectively run an “Office of the CEO” — maintaining context across priorities, decisions, hiring, and key initiatives so nothing slips through the cracks. This role has a seat at the table and operates with a high degree of trust and autonomy.

 - Highly versatile: On any given day, this role might involve anything from supporting hiring and leadership planning, pressure-testing product or GTM strategy, helping move an enterprise deal forward, or driving a cross-functional initiative that helps the company function better.

 - This is a leadership role with wide scope and high expectations — and it’s also one of the fastest ways to grow as an operator. You’ll gain a front-row seat and will be an active partner in building and scaling a company: product decisions, hiring, fundraising, GTM, and operational tradeoffs.

 - Externally facing: This role requires high emotional intelligence and strong judgment. You’ll regularly represent Joyful — and the CEO — in internal and external contexts, including with partners, candidates, customers, and investors. We’re looking for someone who can build trust quickly, read the room, and act with the same care and thoughtfulness as the leadership team.


WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT:

 - This is not an Executive Assistant role, and it’s not a role focused solely on “special projects.” The core of the job is building leverage, operating systems, and alignment as we grow.

We’re deeply invested in your growth and will support you in getting where you want to go. This role is especially well-suited for someone who wants to start a company one day or grow into senior leadership and wants to see the full picture — not just one function.

This role is full-time. We’re looking for someone NYC-based who can be in the office ~3 days per week.

The base pay range for this role is $175,000 – $210,000 per year.




WHAT YOU’LL DO…

 - Partner closely with the CEO as a thought partner on company-wide priorities, decision-making, and execution - making sure that their time is focused on the most impactful and highest-leverage priorities.

 - Maintain full context on what’s in flight across the business — key initiatives, hires, deals, risks — and ensure nothing important slips.

 - Prepare for board meetings, leadership discussions, and high-stakes external conversations.

notes: High-tech roles in AI, data, and software engineering, with a focus on healthcare financial systems.
